evals.expected_output: Classic N+1 query problem: 1 query to get all Posts, then N queries (one per post) to lazy-load each author. Fix: use joinedload or selectinload. Example: session.query(Post).options(joinedload(Post.author)).all(). For large result sets, consider selectinload which uses a separate IN query instead of a JOIN. Also flag: no LIMIT — unbounded query will load entire table.

assertions.text: Identifies the N+1 query problem explicitly
Provides the joinedload or selectinload fix with correct SQLAlchemy syntax
Flags the missing LIMIT as a scalability risk

evals.prompt: We need to add a `status` column to a `payments` table in production (500M rows, accessed 24/7). How should we do this migration safely?
evals.expected_output: Zero-downtime migration strategy: (1) Add column as nullable first — ALTER TABLE payments ADD COLUMN status VARCHAR(20) NULL; (2) Backfill in batches — UPDATE payments SET status='completed' WHERE id BETWEEN x AND y (avoid locking the whole table); (3) Add NOT NULL constraint only after backfill completes (PostgreSQL can do this without table lock if default is set); (4) Add index CONCURRENTLY to avoid table lock; (5) Remove old constraint/default in separate migration. Also: test rollback path — ALTER TABLE DROP COLUMN if needed.

assertions.text: Recommends adding column as nullable first (not NOT NULL immediately)
Recommends batched backfill instead of single UPDATE on 500M rows
Mentions CREATE INDEX CONCURRENTLY to avoid table lock
